Home›About Us

About Mamoratwa Wa Afrika

Rooted in community, guided by compassion, committed to dignity.

Our Story

Born from love for our people.

Mamoratwa Wa Afrika was founded with a singular purpose — to bring dignity, comfort, and compassion to African families during their most difficult moments. Our founders understood that a funeral is not merely a ceremony; it is a final act of love, a celebration of life, and a cultural cornerstone.

Having worked closely with communities across the North West Province for years, we combined our passion and expertise to build a funeral undertaking service that truly reflects the warmth, resilience, and spirit of African people.

Today, we serve families from Zeerust to Mafikeng and beyond — including international repatriations to neighbouring countries like Botswana — with professionalism, grace, and heartfelt dedication.

[ Replace with company / team image ]
What Drives Us

Our Core Values

🕊️
Dignity
Every life deserves to be honoured with the respect and dignity it was lived with. We handle each family's loved one as if they were our own.
🤝
Compassion
We walk alongside families through grief with genuine empathy, patience, and care — offering comfort even in the most overwhelming moments.
🌍
Community
We are proudly rooted in African culture and community. Our service is an expression of ubuntu — I am because we are.
✅
Integrity
Transparent pricing, honest communication, and ethical practice are non-negotiable. Families trust us with everything — we honour that trust.
⭐
Excellence
From our vehicles to our staff to our service execution, we hold ourselves to the highest standard at every step of the process.
🛡️
Reliability
When a family calls us, we are there — 24 hours a day for emergencies. Grief does not keep office hours, and neither do we.
The People Behind MWAU

Meet Our Team

M
Mmamotshegoa
Founder & Director
With over 15 years of experience in community service, Mmamotshegoa founded MWAU to give African families access to dignified, professional funeral care that honours cultural traditions.
T
Tebogo
Operations Manager
Tebogo oversees all branch operations, logistics, and service delivery across Zeerust and Mafikeng, ensuring every family receives consistent, high-quality care.
N
Naledi
Client Relations
Naledi is the warm voice that families hear when they call MWAU. She guides clients through the process with patience, empathy, and thorough knowledge of all our services.
Find Us

Our Branches

Zeerust Branch
43 Klip St, Zeerust, 2865
Mon–Fri: 08:00–17:00
Saturday: 08:00–13:00
Sundays / Public Holidays: Closed
Mafikeng Branch
Office No. 7, Warren Street, Mafikeng
Mon–Fri: 08:00–17:00
Saturday: 08:00–13:00
Sundays / Public Holidays: Closed
Emergency line available 24/7
073 621 3508

Give your loved ones a dignified send off. Rooted in compassion, guided by African values.
